pi_items.source: Task description line states: 'First name: Kwame' which is parsed and stored as metadata
Task description line states: 'Last name: Mensah' which is parsed and stored as metadata
Task description line states: 'Gender: Male' which is parsed and stored as metadata
Task description line states: 'Age: 34 years old' which is parsed and stored as metadata (numeric value)

Analysis of environment setup: seed_bahmni.py explicitly creates 20 test patients with synthetic names from US Census data; credentials are default test credentials (superman/Admin123); task is part of AI benchmark framework; Bahmni installation is containerized test environment

Task description and scripts reference Bahmni; confirmed via web search as real open-source hospital management system (https://www.bahmni.org); over 2 million patient records managed globally via Bahmni
Task description explicitly states these credentials; confirmed in seed_bahmni.py (lines 226-228) and setup_bahmni.sh (lines 14-15) as default test credentials

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic test task for an AI agent benchmark. The patient details (Kwame Mensah, Male, age 34) are test data specified in the task description and stored in metadata. All metadata claims have been verified against the task description—they match exactly. The environment runs a containerized Bahmni (OpenMRS) installation with seeded synthetic test patients and default credentials (superman/Admin123). Bahmni is a real, open-source hospital management system deployed globally, but this instance contains only test/demo data. The task asks an agent to register a new patient (who does not already exist) with the specified details via the Bahmni UI.
